slices.name: tenant-context-walking-skeleton
slices.description: Platform operator creates a tenant and authenticated requests carry tenant context through one protected admin endpoint.
slices.outcome: tenant identity exists and protected APIs can resolve tenant context

slices.name: tenant-admin-management-scope
slices.description: Tenant admin manages products and userbase records scoped to their tenant with audit logs.
slices.outcome: admin management data is tenant-filtered and auditable

slices.name: channel-catalog-capabilities
slices.description: Tenant admin creates channel definitions with provider, country/operator scope, and supported capabilities.
slices.outcome: tenant-owned channels can be selected by future campaigns and service routing

slices.name: channel-credential-binding
slices.description: Tenant admin binds a secret-backed credential reference to a channel without exposing secret material.
slices.outcome: channel routing can resolve active credential references securely

slices.name: tenant-campaign-binding
slices.description: Tenant admin creates and reads campaigns scoped by tenant and bound to a compatible channel.
slices.outcome: campaigns are tenant-scoped and channel-compatible before public use

slices.name: tenant-acquisition-flow
slices.description: End subscriber starts and confirms an acquisition transaction through a tenant campaign.
slices.outcome: transactions, consent, HE, attribution, and next action are tenant-scoped
failure_modes_to_cover: pending transaction reuse across tenant boundary

slices.name: channel-subscription-routing
slices.description: API-integrated partner and acquisition services route optin, confirm, optout, status, MT, and charge through tenant channel configuration.
slices.outcome: subscription-external never uses global TIMWE credentials when tenant/channel is required

slices.name: notification-and-cadence-routing
slices.description: Notifications, message outbox, cadence state, and worker dispatch preserve tenant and channel context.
slices.outcome: notification and cadence workflows are tenant-filtered and channel-aware

slices.name: postback-attribution-routing
slices.description: Ad partner postbacks and attribution rules resolve by tenant, campaign, channel, and provider.
slices.outcome: conversion postbacks are tenant/provider-specific and recoverable through tenant-scoped DLQ

slices.name: tenant-reporting-operations
slices.description: Tenant admin views reports, exports, queues, charging failures, renewal, and health filtered by tenant and channel.
slices.outcome: tenant operations can diagnose platform health without cross-tenant data leakage

slices.name: legacy-data-migration-isolation
slices.description: Platform operator migrates existing global data into a default tenant and verifies tenant isolation constraints.
slices.outcome: existing production data is backfilled with tenant_id and verified before multi-tenant enforcement

slices.name: public-tenant-routing
slices.description: Public landing, campaign, HE, callback, and gateway routes resolve tenant deterministically before work begins.
slices.outcome: public routes cannot choose the wrong tenant when campaign slugs overlap

slices.name: inbound-callback-correlation
slices.description: Inbound TIMWE/MNO/partner callbacks correlate to tenant and channel before updating subscription, notification, charge, or postback state.
slices.outcome: callbacks update only the intended tenant/channel state or quarantine safely

slices.name: admin-portal-tenant-workspace
slices.description: Admin portal exposes a tenant workspace, tenant route guards, platform operator tenant switching, and API tenant error handling.
slices.outcome: tenant admins see and mutate only their tenant resources in the UI

slices.name: platform-ops-secret-observability
slices.description: Platform operator removes unsafe secret patterns, documents rotation, and adds tenant/channel observability labels and dashboards.
slices.outcome: tenant channels operate with safe credentials and visible tenant-specific health

slices.name: partner-channel-onboarding-contracts
slices.description: Partner receives versioned tenant/channel API, callback, credential, sandbox, and postback contracts.
slices.outcome: new tenant channels can be onboarded without bespoke engineering discovery

slices.name: billing-charge-ownership
slices.description: Platform operator decides and proves the tenant-aware owner for charge and billing flows across subscription-external and the disabled billing service.
slices.entrypoint: subscription-external charge endpoints and services/billing transaction endpoints
slices.outcome: charge-capable channels do not split tenant billing state across ambiguous services

slices.name: tenant-claim-and-service-auth-contract
slices.description: Platform services share typed tenant claims, role scopes, trusted service headers, and request-context propagation.
slices.entrypoint: common/auth/auth0jwt, admin middleware, KrakenD trusted header policy
slices.outcome: all protected, public, partner, and worker flows rely on one tenant trust boundary

slices.name: tenant-asset-namespacing
slices.description: Campaign assets and presigned URLs use tenant-scoped object-storage keys and public-safe DTOs.
slices.outcome: landing pages cannot read or overwrite another tenant's campaign media

slices.name: tenant-observability-baseline
slices.description: HTTP services and workers emit safe tenant/channel context in logs, metrics, traces, and health signals.
slices.entrypoint: HTTP middleware logs, Prometheus metrics, worker health
slices.outcome: operators can diagnose tenant issues without high-cardinality or PII leakage

slices.name: Release verification matrix
slices.notes: Blocked by unresolved release-verification blockers: webspa-admin gitlink is not fetchable, compose runtime still needs schema provisioning and real env/provider values, dependency remediation needs approval, and primary main diverges from origin/main.

webspa-admin submodule verification
